title: 🏛️ Organization Copilot Leading Indicators [Edu]
description: Organization-level Copilot leading indicators — adoption, code volume, interactions, and full pull request activity (all API fields) — for the selected organization.

list.name: organization_id
list.label: Organization
list.definition: SELECT DISTINCT organization_id FROM copilot_organization_daily WHERE organization_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY organization_id
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT organization_id FROM copilot_organization_daily WHERE organization_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY organization_id

panels.description: Data mode banner — shows whether this dashboard displays live GitHub data (green), synthetic seed data (amber), or demo data (blue).

targets.rawSql: SELECT COALESCE('Live: ' || (SELECT value FROM app_config WHERE key = 'org') || '/' || (SELECT value FROM app_config WHERE key = 'repo'), 'Not yet synced') AS "Data Source"

options.content:
# 🏛️ Organization Copilot Leading Indicators

**Organization-Scope Copilot Adoption, Code Volume, and Pull Request Activity**

Use the **Organization** dropdown above to select an organization within the enterprise.

This dashboard surfaces organization-aggregate data from the GitHub Copilot Metrics Reports API (`organization-28-day` report). It shows how a single organization is using Copilot — broken down by adoption metrics (active users), code volume (lines suggested/added/deleted), interaction counts, and pull request activity (creation, review, merge, suggestions — including activity performed by Copilot itself).

## What it Measures

Each metric below corresponds to a field in the [Copilot usage metrics API](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics). The Pull Request Activity section reflects every `pull_requests.*` field documented as **API only** by GitHub.

## Why it Matters

Organization-level metrics let you compare divisions, business units, or product teams against each other. Look for orgs lagging in adoption or PR activity that may benefit from targeted enablement.

> ⚠️ **Note on scope-level differences:** Per GitHub's documentation, organization- and enterprise-level reports may show different totals due to differences in user deduplication and attribution timing.

panels.description: 🏛️ Organization Copilot Leading Indicators [Edu]

options.content: ## Adoption

panels.title: Daily Active Users (peak in range) — Learning Guide
Daily Active Users (peak in range)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT MAX(daily_active_users) AS "Daily Active Users (peak in range)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `day_totals[].daily_active_users` | Table: `copilot_organization_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].daily_active_users`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Maximum daily active user count observed during the selected window. A user is "daily active" if they had any Copilot activity that calendar day.

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Trends upward as adoption grows | Flat or declining — investigate adoption blockers |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT MAX(daily_active_users) AS "Daily Active Users (peak in range)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

Weekly Active Users (peak in range)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT MAX(weekly_active_users) AS "Weekly Active Users (peak in range)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `day_totals[].weekly_active_users` | Table: `copilot_organization_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].weekly_active_users`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Maximum weekly active user count observed in the window — number of unique users active during a rolling 7-day window.

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Steadily climbing or stable near seat count | Far below seat count — large idle population |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT MAX(weekly_active_users) AS "Weekly Active Users (peak in range)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `day_totals[].monthly_active_users` | Table: `copilot_organization_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].monthly_active_users`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Maximum monthly active user count in the window. Represents licensed users active in the rolling 28-day period.

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Approaches assigned-seat count | Significantly below seats — license waste |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT MAX(monthly_active_users) AS "Monthly Active Users"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `day_totals[].monthly_active_agent_users` | Table: `copilot_organization_daily`

### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].monthly_active_agent_users`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Maximum count of users who used Copilot agent mode in the IDE during the rolling 28-day window.

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Growing as agent adoption matures | Near zero — users not exploring agent capabilities |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT MAX(monthly_active_agent_users) AS "Monthly Active Agent Users"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].monthly_active_chat_users`

`copilot_organization_daily`

Maximum count of users who used Copilot chat (any mode) in the IDE during the rolling 28-day window.

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT MAX(monthly_active_chat_users) AS "Monthly Active Chat Users"

  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].daily_active_cli_users`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Maximum count of users who used `gh copilot` (CLI) on any single day in the window. Independent of IDE activity.

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Non-zero indicates CLI adoption | Always zero — CLI not promoted to users |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT MAX(daily_active_cli_users) AS "Daily Active CLI Users (peak in range)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```
## Code Volume

panels.title: Lines of Code Suggested to Add (28d) — Learning Guide
Lines of Code Suggested to Add (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum) AS "Lines of Code Suggested to Add (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `day_totals[].loc_suggested_to_add_sum` | Table: `copilot_organization_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].loc_suggested_to_add_sum`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Total lines of code Copilot suggested to add (completions, inline chat, chat panel — excludes agent edits).

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Grows with active usage | Flat — usage stalled |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum) AS "Lines of Code Suggested to Add (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `day_totals[].loc_suggested_to_delete_sum` | Table: `copilot_organization_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].loc_suggested_to_delete_sum`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Total lines Copilot suggested to delete (future support planned — may report 0).

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Non-zero in agent/edit-heavy orgs | N/A — feature still rolling out |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(loc_suggested_to_delete_sum) AS "Lines of Code Suggested to Delete (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

Lines of Code Added (28d)

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `day_totals[].loc_added_sum` | Table: `copilot_organization_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].loc_added_sum`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Total lines actually added to the editor — accepted completions, applied chat code blocks, and agent/edit mode writes.

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Significant fraction of `loc_suggested_to_add_sum` | Far below suggested — low acceptance |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(loc_added_sum) AS "Lines of Code Added (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].loc_deleted_sum`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Total lines deleted from the editor (currently from agent edits).

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Indicates agent/edit mode in use | Zero — agent/edit mode not adopted |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(loc_deleted_sum) AS "Lines of Code Deleted (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```
## Interactions

panels.title: Total User-Initiated Interactions (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(user_initiated_interaction_count) AS "Total User-Initiated Interactions (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `day_totals[].user_initiated_interaction_count` | Table: `copilot_organization_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].user_initiated_interaction_count`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Sum of explicit prompts users sent to Copilot — chat messages, accept actions, etc. Does not count opening the panel or switching modes.

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Grows week-over-week | Flat or declining — explore blockers |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(user_initiated_interaction_count) AS "Total User-Initiated Interactions (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.title: Code Generation Activities (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(code_generation_activity_count) AS "Code Generation Activities (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `day_totals[].code_generation_activity_count` | Table: `copilot_organization_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].code_generation_activity_count`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Number of distinct Copilot output events generated — each code block from a single prompt counts separately.

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Higher than interaction count (one prompt → many blocks) | Lower than interactions — short responses or low engagement |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(code_generation_activity_count) AS "Code Generation Activities (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.title: Code Acceptance Activities (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(code_acceptance_activity_count) AS "Code Acceptance Activities (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `day_totals[].code_acceptance_activity_count` | Table: `copilot_organization_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].code_acceptance_activity_count`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Number of suggestions or code blocks accepted by users — apply-to-file, insert-at-cursor, copy button. Excludes manual OS clipboard.

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Strong fraction of generation activities | Very low ratio — relevance issues |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(code_acceptance_activity_count) AS "Code Acceptance Activities (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```
## Pull Request Activity

panels.title: PRs Created (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM((pull_requests->>'total_created')::bigint) AS "PRs Created (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND pull_requests ? 'total_created'

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `day_totals[].pull_requests.total_created` | Table: `copilot_organization_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].pull_requests.total_created`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Total pull requests created on each day in the window. Creation is a one-time event — each PR counted only on its creation day.

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Steady or growing PR throughput | Sharp drop — investigate workflow blockers |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM((pull_requests->>'total_created')::bigint) AS "PRs Created (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND pull_requests ? 'total_created'
```

panels.title: PRs Reviewed (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM((pull_requests->>'total_reviewed')::bigint) AS "PRs Reviewed (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND pull_requests ? 'total_reviewed'

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `day_totals[].pull_requests.total_reviewed` | Table: `copilot_organization_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].pull_requests.total_reviewed`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Pull requests reviewed each day. A PR may be counted on multiple days if it receives reviews on multiple days; once per day per PR.

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Tracks closely with PRs created | Reviews lagging — review bottleneck |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM((pull_requests->>'total_reviewed')::bigint) AS "PRs Reviewed (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND pull_requests ? 'total_reviewed'
```

panels.title: PRs Merged (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM((pull_requests->>'total_merged')::bigint) AS "PRs Merged (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND pull_requests ? 'total_merged'

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `day_totals[].pull_requests.total_merged` | Table: `copilot_organization_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].pull_requests.total_merged`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Pull requests merged each day. One-time event — each PR counted only on merge day.

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Consistent flow into main branch | Significantly lower than created — backlog growing |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM((pull_requests->>'total_merged')::bigint) AS "PRs Merged (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND pull_requests ? 'total_merged'
```

panels.title: Median Minutes to Merge (28d avg) — Learning Guide
Median Minutes to Merge (28d avg)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT AVG((pull_requests->>'median_minutes_to_merge')::numeric) AS "Median Minutes to Merge (28d avg)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND pull_requests ? 'median_minutes_to_merge'

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `day_totals[].pull_requests.median_minutes_to_merge` | Table: `copilot_organization_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].pull_requests.median_minutes_to_merge`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Average of the daily median minutes between PR creation and merge for PRs merged that day. Median reduces the impact of long-running outliers.

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Lower is better — fast merge cycle | Climbing — review or CI bottleneck |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT AVG((pull_requests->>'median_minutes_to_merge')::numeric) AS "Median Minutes to Merge (28d avg)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND pull_requests ? 'median_minutes_to_merge'
```

panels.title: PR Review Suggestions (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM((pull_requests->>'total_suggestions')::bigint) AS "PR Review Suggestions (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND pull_requests ? 'total_suggestions'

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `day_totals[].pull_requests.total_suggestions` | Table: `copilot_organization_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].pull_requests.total_suggestions`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Total PR review suggestions generated each day, regardless of author (humans + Copilot).

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Active code review culture | Near zero — reviewers leaving narrative comments only |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM((pull_requests->>'total_suggestions')::bigint) AS "PR Review Suggestions (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND pull_requests ? 'total_suggestions'
```

panels.title: PR Applied Suggestions (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM((pull_requests->>'total_applied_suggestions')::bigint) AS "PR Applied Suggestions (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND pull_requests ? 'total_applied_suggestions'

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `day_totals[].pull_requests.total_applied_suggestions` | Table: `copilot_organization_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].pull_requests.total_applied_suggestions`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Total PR review suggestions that were applied each day, regardless of author.

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Strong fraction of total suggestions | Low ratio — suggestions ignored or low-quality |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM((pull_requests->>'total_applied_suggestions')::bigint) AS "PR Applied Suggestions (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND pull_requests ? 'total_applied_suggestions'
```

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Indicates Copilot code review adoption | Zero — Copilot reviewer not enabled or assigned |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM((pull_requests->>'total_reviewed_by_copilot')::bigint) AS "PRs Reviewed by Copilot (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND pull_requests ? 'total_reviewed_by_copilot'
```

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].pull_requests.total_merged_created_by_copilot`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Number of Copilot-authored PRs that merged each day. One-time event.

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Healthy fraction of Copilot-created PRs | Many created, few merged — Copilot PR quality issue |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM((pull_requests->>'total_merged_created_by_copilot')::bigint) AS "PRs Merged that Copilot Created (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND pull_requests ? 'total_merged_created_by_copilot'
```

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `day_totals[].pull_requests.median_minutes_to_merge_copilot_authored` | Table: `copilot_organization_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].pull_requests.median_minutes_to_merge_copilot_authored`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Average of the daily median minutes from creation to merge for Copilot-authored PRs merged that day.

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Comparable to or faster than overall median | Much higher — Copilot PRs need extensive rework |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT AVG((pull_requests->>'median_minutes_to_merge_copilot_authored')::numeric) AS "Median Minutes to Merge — Copilot Authored (28d avg)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND pull_requests ? 'median_minutes_to_merge_copilot_authored'
```

### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10) → `day_totals[].pull_requests.total_copilot_applied_suggestions`

### Database Table

`copilot_organization_daily`

### How It's Calculated

PR review suggestions generated by Copilot that were applied each day.

### How to Interpret

| Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|
| Strong fraction of Copilot suggestions accepted | Low ratio — Copilot reviewer suggestions ignored |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM((pull_requests->>'total_copilot_applied_suggestions')::bigint) AS "Copilot Applied PR Suggestions (28d)"
FROM copilot_organization_daily
WHERE organization_id = '${organization_id}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND pull_requests ? 'total_copilot_applied_suggestions'
```
